title: GKIDS' IMAX 4K Screening of Kiki's Delivery Service Opens at #7 in U.S. Box Office
ai_summary: GKIDS' IMAX screening of Studio Ghibli's Kiki's Delivery Service, a 4K restoration, opened at #7 in the United States box office during its opening weekend. The film earned an estimated $1,668,734 over 249 screens from Friday to Sunday. It debuted with $493,004 on Friday and $652,408 on Saturday, followed by an estimated $523,322 on Sunday. The screening took place on March 13 in the U.S., where it was presented in Japanese with English subtitles and English dubbing. This re-release follows GKIDS' previous participation at Studio Ghibli Fest 2023. Meanwhile, the Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle - Akaza Sairai film dropped from #10 to #18 in its second weekend of release, earning around $335,000 from Friday to Sunday and a total of over $136 million in the U.S.
og_content: News GKIDS' IMAX 4K Screening of Kiki's Delivery Service Opens at #7 in U.S. Box Office GKIDS' IMAX screening of the 4K restoration of Studio Ghibli's Kiki's Delivery Service film ranked at #7 in the United States box office in its opening weekend. The film earned an estimated US$1,668,734 over 249 screens in its first three days from Friday to Sunday. The film earned US$493,004 on Friday, US$652,408 on Saturday, and an estimated US$523,322 on Sunday. GKIDS' screening opened on March 13. The film is screening with Japanese audio and English subtitles, as well as with the English language dub. GKIDS previously screened the film in the United States during its Studio Ghibli Fest 2023 event. GKIDS announced in December plans to partner with IMAX to bring 4K restorations of Studio Ghibli films to North American theaters in 2026. Studio Ghibli animator Atsushi Okui is overseeing the creation of the IMAX versions that are being made from new 4K restorations of the films. The re-release of Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle - Akaza Sairai, the first film in the Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle trilogy, dropped from #10 to #18 in its second weekend, earning an estimated US$335,000 from Friday to Sunday. The film has now earned a total of US$136,632,141 in the United States. The film returned to theaters in North America on March 6. Crunchyroll and Sony Pictures Entertainment also brought the film to SCREENX multi-projection screens in North America for the first time. The film ranked at #10 on the first weekend of its re-release, and earned US$1,300,000 in its first three days. The film first screened in the U.S. and Canada on September 12, 2025. Sources: Box Office Mojo ( link 2 , link 3), Deadline (Jill Goldsmith)
snippet: Re-release of Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle - Akaza Sairai also at #18

title: Marriage Toxin Anime Reveals Opening Song by Artist Yurina Hirate
ai_summary: The anime adaptation of Jōmyaku and Mizuki Yoda's Marriage Toxin manga announced a series of "Making of Marriage Toxin" videos that highlight various aspects of the production process. The first video in the series reveals the opening theme song "Kill or Kiss," performed by artist Yurina Hirate, for the anime adaptation directed by Motonobu Hori at Bones Film. The staff includes Kimiko Ueno as scriptwriter and series composer Taisei Iwasaki and Yuma Yamaguchi.
og_content: News Marriage Toxin Anime Reveals Opening Song by Artist Yurina Hirate The staff for the television anime for Jōmyaku and artist Mizuki Yoda's Marriage Toxin manga announced on Monday that the staff will release a series of "Making of Marriage Toxin" videos highlighting the anime's character design, color design, photography, action scene animation, and sound design. To start the series of videos, the anime's staff started streaming a digest of the upcoming videos titled "Making of Marriage Toxin Vol. 0." The video also reveals and previews the anime's opening theme song "Kill or Kiss" by Yurina Hirate. The series stars: Haruki Ishiya as Hikaru Gero Shion Wakayama as Mei Kinosaki Anna Nagase as Kyoko Himekawa Mariya Ise as Shizuku Ushio Asaki Yuikawa as Shiori Ureshino Sōma Saitō as Genya Naruko Hinano Shirahama as Kimie Arashiyama Simba Tsuchiya as Piichi Nakagawa Haruka Shiraishi as Akari Gero Isamu Yūsen as Toshiki Hanamaki Motonobu Hori ( Carole & Tuesday ) is directing the anime at Bones Film with production supervision by BONES. Kimiko Ueno ( Astro Note ) is in charge of the script and series composition. Taisei Iwasaki (BELLE) and Yuma Yamaguchi ( Metallic Rouge ) are composing the music. Kōhei Tokuoka ( The Fragrant Flower Blooms With Dignity ) is serving as chief animation director and is also the character designer. Additional staff members include: Sub Character Design: Yurika Sako Costume Design: Kotono Prop Design: Ayumi Nagaki, Ryō Hirata Art Design: Toshiki Amata Art: Bihou, Inc. Color Design: Hiroko Umezaki Photography Director: Gō Kanbayashi 3DCG: SANZIGEN Inc. CG Animation Director: Taiki Nagashima Editing: Kumiko Sakamoto Sound Director: Haru Yamada Sound Effects: Tomokazu Mii Shueisha describes the anime: For centuries, the deadly arts have been perfected by those known as the Masters. Among them, the Poison Masters of the Five Great Families hold the most fearsome power and influence. Hikaru Gero, heir to the Poison Masters' Gero Family, has lived his entire life in the underworld, far removed from love or marriage. But when the Gero Family head decrees that Hikaru's sister must bear an heir to preserve the bloodline – by force if necessary – Hikaru resolves to protect her at all costs. It is then that Hikaru encounters Mei Kinosaki, a brilliant marriage swindler who becomes the target of his latest job. Realizing that the only way of solving the family succession problem and protecting his sister is through marriage, Hikaru impulsively asks Mei for help in finding a bride. “That's the first time anyone's ever proposed like that.” And so, with the cunning marriage swindler Mei as his advisor, elite assassin Hikaru embarks on the most challenging mission of his life: finding a wife! Their goal: the ultimate marriage. This is the world's toughest mission for love, brought to you by the ultimate duo – an assassin and a marriage swindler! To win love, you've got to play with poison. A high-stakes mission for the ultimate duo begins! Jōmyaku and Yoda launched the manga on the Shonen Jump+ app in April 2022. Shueisha will publish the manga's 17th volume on April 3. Viz Media licensed the manga. The manga is also in English on Shueisha's MANGA Plus app. Sources: Marriage Toxin anime's website, Comic Natalie
snippet: "Kill or Kiss" opening song revealed in 1st "Making of Marriage Toxin" video

title: New Assassination Classroom Film's Video Reveals Insert Song
ai_summary: The official website for the Assassination Classroom film recently posted a promotional video revealing an insert song titled "Machine Gun to Rashinban" performed by various characters from the original series. The cast will include actors reprising their roles as in previous seasons of the television anime. Directed by Masaki Kitamura with scripts by Makoto Uezu, the film features new episodes not included in earlier versions and is scheduled to open on March 20, 2026 in Japan through Avex Film Labels. The movie is described as both a celebration of the manga's 10th anniversary and a continuation of its main story, which ran from 2012 to 2016.
og_content: News New Assassination Classroom Film's Video Reveals Insert Song The official website for the Gekijōban Ansatsu Kyōshitsu Minna no Jikan (Assassination Classroom the Movie: Everybody's Time) anime film posted a new promotional video for the film on Monday. The video reveals and previews the film's insert song "Machine Gun to Rashinban" (Machine Gun and Compass), performed by Mai Fuchigami, Aya Suzaki, Nobuhiko Okamoto, Ryōta Ōsaka, and Shintarō Asanuma as their respective characters, under the unit name 3-nen E-gumi Utatan, the unit name also used for the television anime songs. Previously announced new cast includes: The cast members from the previous television anime will reprise their roles for the film. Masaki Kitamura (After-School Hanako-kun, Gundam Evolve) is directing the film at Lerche. Makoto Uezu returns to pen the script for the film, and Aya Higami (After-School Hanako-kun) is designing the characters. Singer-songwriter Sora Tomonari will perform the film's theme song "Teacher." The film will open in Japan on March 20, 2026 with Avex Film Labels distributing. The new film contains a full collection of episodes that were left out of the anime due to time constraints. The original manga's author Yūsei Matsui stated that the film is not just a 10th anniversary project, but is also a continuation of the main story. Matsui's Assassination Classroom manga ran in Weekly Shonen Jump from 2012 to 2016. The manga inspired a 22-episode television anime series that aired in 2015, and a 25-episode second season that aired in 2016. An anime film then opened in Japan in November 2016. Toonami began airing the anime's first season in August 2020, and started airing the second season in 2022. Assassination Classroom also inspired two live-action films that opened in Japan in March 2015 and March 2016, respectively. Source: Gekijōban Ansatsu Kyōshitsu Minna no Jikan anime film's website, Comic Natalie
snippet: 3-nen E-gumi Utatan performs insert song "Machine Gun to Rashinban"

title: Witch Hat Atelier Anime Casts Kotono Mitsuishi
ai_summary: The upcoming anime adaptation of Kamome Shirahama's Witch Hat Atelier manga will feature Kotono Mitsuishi as Alaira, a character who dreams of becoming a wizard despite her lack of magical aptitude. The series stars Rena Motomura as Coco and Natsuki Hanae as Qifrey. Additional casts include Hibiku Yamamura, Haruki, Hika Tsukishiro, Yūichi Nakamura, Ayumu Watanabe, who will also direct the anime at BUG FILMS. Launched in 2016, the manga has won several awards and been listed on top graphic novel and manga lists for male readers. The series is set to premiere on April 6th with opening and ending theme songs provided by Eve and suis, and Nakamura Hak respectively.
og_content: News Witch Hat Atelier Anime Casts Kotono Mitsuishi The staff for the upcoming television anime of Kamome Shirahama's Witch Hat Atelier manga announced on Sunday that Kotono Mitsuishi will play Alaira. The manga's story centers on Coco, a girl who lives in a little village, and who has always dreamed of becoming a wizard. But being born without magical aptitude meant that she could never become a wizard, and could never hope to witness magic's spark. She gave up on her dream. But one day, a traveling wizard named Kiefley came to her village, and she happened to see him performing magic. The anime stars Rena Motomura as Coco and Natsuki Hanae as Qifrey. The additional cast members include: Hibiku Yamamura as Agott Kurumi Haruki as Tetia Hika Tsukishiro as Richeh Yūichi Nakamura as Olruggio Misaki Kuno as Brushbuddy (Fudemushi) Mitsuki Saiga as Iguin Ayumu Watanabe (Ace Attorney, Doraemon films) is directing the anime at BUG FILMS. Hiroaki Kojima (animation producer for Komi Can't Communicate) is producing. Kairi Unabara (Pokémon Evolutions) is designing the characters and is also serving as chief animation director. Yuka Kitamura (Elden Ring) is composing the music. Additional staff members include: Assistant Director: Jun Shinohara Series Script Supervisor: Hiroshi Seko Art Director: Ryōta Gotō Color Key Artist: Naomi Nakano Compositing Director of Photography: Tadashi Kitaoka Eve and suis perform the opening theme song "Kaze no Anthem feat. suis from yorushika" (Anthem of Wind), and Nakamura Hak performs the ending theme song "Tada Utsukushii no Noroi" (Just a Beautiful Curse). The anime will premiere on April 6 at 11:00 p.m. on Tokyo MX and on other channels. Crunchyroll will stream the anime as it airs. Shirahama launched the manga in Kodansha's Morning two magazine in July 2016. Kodansha published the manga's 15th compiled book volume on November 21. Kodansha USA Publishing began releasing the series in English in April 2019. The series was nominated for the Best Comic award at Angoulême International Comics Festival in January 2019. The manga was also nominated for the 11th Manga Taisho awards in 2018. The series ranked in the top 10 on the list of the best manga for male readers in the 2018 edition of Takarajimasha's Kono Manga ga Sugoi! (This Manga Is Amazing!) guidebook. The manga ranked on American Library Association's (ALA's) Young Adult Library Services Association (YALSA) Top Ten Great Graphic Novels for Teens list in 2020. The series won Best Manga at the Harvey Awards in October 2020, and also won the award in 2025. The manga has 7 million copies in circulation worldwide. Sources: Witch Hat Atelier anime's website, Mantan Web
snippet: Character visual for Alaira revealed

title: The Detective is Already Dead Season 2 Delayed to October 2026
ai_summary: The second season of the television anime "The Detective is Already Dead" (Tantei wa Mō, Shindeiru) has been delayed from its original July 2026 premiere to October 2026 due to production issues. The series was previously announced in July 2022 and follows Kimihiko as he assists Siesta, a detective who mysteriously dies after years of adventuring. Directed by Manabu Kurihara at ENGI, the anime is now again led by Deko Akao for scripts and Yōsuke Itō for character designs. The series stars Arata Nagai, Saki Miyashita, Ayana Taketatsu, among others.
og_content: News The Detective is Already Dead Season 2 Delayed to October 2026 The official X (formerly Twitter) account for the television anime of Nigojū and Umibōzu's The Detective Is Already Dead (Tantei wa Mō, Shindeiru.) light novel series announced on Sunday that the second season will now premiere in October 2026 due to production issues. Kadokawa previously announced the second season would air in July 2026. Manabu Kurihara returns to direct the second season at ENGI. Deko Akao is again in charge of series scripts, and Yōsuke Itō is again designing the characters. The returning cast includes: Arata Nagai as Kimihiko Kimizuka Saki Miyashita as Siesta Ayana Taketatsu as Nagisa Natsunagi Kanon Takao as Yui Saikawa Saho Shirasu as Charlotte Arisaka Anderson The anime's second season was first announced in July 2022. The anime's first season premiered with a one-hour special in July 2021 and aired for 12 episodes. Funimation began streaming the anime in July 2021, and also streamed an English dub. Funimation describes the story: The series follows Kimizuka Kimihiko, a young man who is always getting into trouble, as he finds himself the assistant of Siesta, a globe-trotting detective, and the two of them solve a series of mysteries. But after years of adventuring and saving lives, Siesta mysteriously dies, though her legacy will shape the lives of Kimihiko and others as they move forward. Nigojū published the first volume in the light novel series with illustrations by Umibōzu in November 2019 under Kadokawa's MF Bunko J imprint. The novel won the 15th MF Bunko J newcomer award in 2019. Kadokawa published the 13th volume on July 25. Yen Press is publishing the original novels in English. Hoko Hinachi and Minori Chigusa launched a new spinoff novel titled Charlotte wa Tada, Jiken o Tokitai. Tantei wa Mō, Shindeiru. Code:RED in May 2024. Nigojū is credited for the original work and as a supervisor for the novel. Mugiko launched the manga adaptation of the novels in Kadokawa's Monthly Comic Alive magazine in May 2020, and ended it in October 2023. Kadokawa published the manga's sixth and final compiled book volume in October 2023. Source: The Detective Is Already Dead anime's X/Twitter account via MyAnimeList
snippet: New season initially slated for July

title: New Battle Spirits TV Anime's Teaser Unveils Cast, Staff, Fall Debut
ai_summary: The new TV anime series "Battle Spirits Re:Zekkai no Kū" by Bandai Namco Pictures has unveiled its main cast and staff members through a dedicated website, revealing the teaser promotional video and premiere date. The anime stars Taichi Kusano as Kū Bashin, Daiki Hamano as Zetsu Ajara, with Masashi Kudo directing. Set to debut in fall, the series is part of Bandai's Battle Spirits multimedia franchise, which began with a trading card game launched in 2008 and has continued through various anime series released annually since 2008.
og_content: News New Battle Spirits TV Anime's Teaser Unveils Cast, Staff, Fall Debut A website opened on Saturday to reveal the Battle Spirits [Re] Zekkai no Kū title for the new television anime series in Bandai's Battle Spirits multimedia franchise. The website also revealed the anime's teaser promotional video, teaser visual, main cast and staff members, and fall premiere. The anime will star: Taichi Kusano as Kū Bashin, a cheerful, positive, sincere fan of Battle Spirits who wants to become stronger Daiki Hamano as Zetsu Ajara, a cool, unapproachable rōnin , feared as an Eternal Battler master assassin Masashi Kudo (Terminator Zero, Sanrio Boys, Hayate the Combat Butler! Cuties) is directing the anime at Bandai Namco Pictures and is also credited for the original character designs. Toshizo Nemoto (Inu X Boku Secret Service, Metallic Rouge, Log Horizon two seasons) is in charge of series scripts. Bandai Namco Pictures is also credited for planning the production. Bandai launched its original Battle Spirits trading card game in 2008. In the game, cards represent dragons, demons, birds, insects, and other spirits that the players can control in battles. Bandai imported the game to the United States in 2009. Battle Spirits: Connected Battlers, the latest video game in the franchise, launched for PlayStation 4 and Nintendo Switch in April 2022. The franchise launched the 50-episode Battle Spirits: Shōnen Toppa Bashin anime series in September 2008. A new television anime series aired almost every year afterwards until 2016-2017's Battle Spirits Double Drive . A three-episode net anime series titled Battle Spirits: Saga Brave then premiered in June 2019. Battle Spirits: Kakumei no Galett launched in August 2020. Battle Spirits: Mirage, the most recent anime in the franchise, premiered online in October 2021. Source: Battle Spirits [Re] Zekkai no Kū anime's website via Ota-suke
snippet: Taichi Kusano, Daiki Hamano star in Battle Spirits [Re] Zekkai no Kū directed by Masashi Kudo

title: Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring Anime Casts Kana Hanazawa, Nao Tōyama
ai_summary: Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring anime announced two new cast members: Kana Hanazawa as Kōbai Yukiyanagi and Nao Tōyama as Nazuna, who will appear in the first episode. The anime features characters voiced by these actors and is directed by Kenji Ito from Wit Studio. It was originally serialized in a novel series written by Kana Akatsuki, which has also been adapted into manga formats.
og_content: News Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring Anime Casts Kana Hanazawa, Nao Tōyama The official website for the television anime of Kana Akatsuki's Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring novel series announced two more cast members on Sunday. Kana Hanazawa (left in image below) is voicing the character Kōbai Yukiyanagi, and Nao Tōyama is voicing the character Nazuna (right). Tōyama's character Nazuna will appear in the anime's first episode. Tokyo MX, Tochigi TV, Gunma TV, and BS11 channels (as well as on various streaming services in Japan) at 24:00 JST (effectively, March 29 at 12:00 a.m. JST). The anime will then air on MBS, CBC TV, RKB Mainichi Broadcasting, and Hokkaido Broadcasting at a later time. The anime stars: Yuka Nukui as Hinagiku Kayō Yoshino Aoyama as Sakura Himedaka Sumire Uesaka as Ruri Hazakura Ranko Baba as Ayame Hazakura Hime Sawada as Nadeshiko Iwaizuki Taku Yashiro as Rindō Azami Shōgo Sakata as Rōsei Kantsubaki Satoshi Hino as Itechō Kangetsu Ken Yamamoto ( Uma Musume Pretty Derby: Beginning of a New Era , Pokémon: Hisuian Snow ) is directing the series at Wit Studio. Kazuhiro Furuhashi (director for Mobile Suit Gundam UC , Getbackers , Spy×Family ) is the animation adviser. Ayumu Hisao ( I'm in Love with the Villainess , Kono Oto Tomare!: Sounds of Life ) is overseeing the series scripts, and Namiko Torii is the character designer. kensuke ushio is composing the music. vocaloid producer and composer Orangestar both produced and performed the opening theme song "Petals feat. Kase" and the ending theme song "Hana Ikada feat. Kase." Other staff members include: Visual Development, Image Board: Satomi Maiya / Yūtarō Kubo Art Director: Yūsuke Takeda (Bamboo) Color Key Artist: Ayaka Nakamura (Wit Studio) Compositing Director of Photography: Keisuke Nozawa (Graphinica Sappporo Studio) Editing: Keisuke Yanagi / ACE Sound Production: Tohokushinsha Sound Director: Eriko Kimura (Tohokushinsha) Animation Producer: Joe Ōtani Line Producer: Keisuke Satō Yen Press has licensed the overall Agents of the Four Seasons novel series, and it describes the first volume of Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring : Once upon a time, there was Winter. Winter was once the only season in the world-but such an existence was too lonely to bear, and so it created Spring to love. Before long, the earth wished for more time to rest in the cycle, and Summer and Autumn were born. The ones who carry the cycle are called the . Hinagiku, the Agent of Spring, disappeared from this land ten years ago, taking the season of spring with her. Now, after incredible hardship, she has returned to restore the cycle to its proper state-and, as in the myth passed down since the dawn of time, she sends her love to Winter. Agents of the Four Seasons The novel series currently consists of two Dance of Spring novels, two Dance of Summer novels, the The Archer of Dawn novel, two Dance of Autumn novels, and the Tasogare no Ite (Archer of Dusk) novel. Suoh illustrates the novels. The novels topped the 2022 edition of Takarajimasha's Kono Light Novel ga Sugoi! (This Light Novel Is Amazing!) guidebook's New Title category in 2021. Nappa Komatsuda launched a manga adaptation of Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring in Hakusensha's LaLa magazine in July 2022. Hakusensha published the sixth compiled book volume on August 5. Yen Press is releasing the manga in English. Yuriko Asami launched a separate manga titled Shunka Shūtō Daikōsha Moka Momoyo ( Agents of the Four Seasons : Hundred Songs, Hundred Leaves) in May 2023 on the KadoComi website. Kadokawa released the manga's second volume in December 2024. Akatsuki's Violet Evergarden novel series won the grand prize in the fifth Kyoto Animation Award program's novel category in 2014. Akatsuki published the novels under Kyoto Animation's KA Esuma Bunko label. The novels inspired a television anime in 2018, a side story anime in 2019, and a theatrical anime sequel in 2020. Sources: Agents of the Four Seasons: Dance of Spring anime's website , Comic Natalie
snippet: Anime debuts on March 28
